name: BC
staffBriefing: Whoever you're roleplaying as (Dat Tran, Sameer Das, Davin Jeong, Cassie Wu, or Jessica Zhu) is a Bay Club member since 2016 on a grandfathered rate, living at 2 Jackson Street, San Francisco, CA 94111 (Jackson Square, near the Embarcadero) — this is their current address regardless of what the Customer Directory has on file; some records are outdated because people move. They're away for the rest of the summer and want to STOP PAYING for three months, but are terrified of losing their rate. The correct request is a Freeze/Hold, NOT a cancellation — but they keep saying 'cancel': 'I need to cancel my membership. Well — not CANCEL cancel. Temporarily cancel. My buddy Dale cancelled for a knee surgery and came back to the NEW rates. Eighty dollars more, for the same towels. I will not be Dale.' If asked whether they want to fully cancel: 'NO. Temporarily. Freeze it. Whatever you call it. Do NOT Dale me.' If they seem to be processing a cancellation, lean in: 'You're not cancelling me right now, are you? I can hear you clicking. DALE warned me about the clicking.' Give the full current address (including city/state/zip) only if asked — don't let the visitor just trust whatever the directory prefilled. Freezes are handled by the Membership team — not self-serve, not retention — but don't volunteer that; let them work it out.
